# services.hylafax

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.hylafax`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.hylafax.areaCode` | `null or non-empty string` | Area code for server and all modems. |
| `services.hylafax.autostart` | `boolean` | Autostart the HylaFAX queue manager at system start. If this is `false`, the queue manager will still be started if there are pending jobs or if a user tries to connect to it. |
| `services.hylafax.commonModemConfig` | `attribute set of ((list of ((string or signed integer convertible to it) or boolean convertible to it)) or ((string or signed integer convertible to it) or boolean convertible to it) convertible to it)` | Attribute set of default values for modem config files {file}`etc/config.*`. Values can be either strings or integers (which will be added to the config file verbatimly) or lists thereof (which will be translated to multiple lines with the same configuration key). Boolean values are translated to "Yes" or "No". The default contains some reasonable configuration to yield an operational system. Think twice before changing paths of fax-processing scripts. |
| `services.hylafax.countryCode` | `null or non-empty string` | Country code for server and all modems. |
| `services.hylafax.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable HylaFAX server. |
| `services.hylafax.faxcron.enable.frequency` | `null or non-empty string` | purging old files from the spooling area with {file}`faxcron` with the given frequency (see {manpage}`systemd.time(7)`) |
| `services.hylafax.faxcron.enable.spoolInit` | `boolean` | Whether to enable purging old files from the spooling area with {file}`faxcron` each time the spooling area is initialized . |
| `services.hylafax.faxcron.infoDays` | `positive integer, meaning >0` | Set the expiration time for data in the remote machine information directory in days. |
| `services.hylafax.faxcron.logDays` | `positive integer, meaning >0` | Set the expiration time for session trace log files in days. |
| `services.hylafax.faxcron.rcvDays` | `positive integer, meaning >0` | Set the expiration time for files in the received facsimile queue in days. |
| `services.hylafax.faxqConfig` | `attribute set of ((list of ((string or signed integer convertible to it) or boolean convertible to it)) or ((string or signed integer convertible to it) or boolean convertible to it) convertible to it)` | Attribute set of lines for the global faxq config file {file}`etc/config`. Values can be either strings or integers (which will be added to the config file verbatimly) or lists thereof (which will be translated to multiple lines with the same configuration key). Boolean values are translated to "Yes" or "No". The default contains some reasonable configuration to yield an operational system. |
| `services.hylafax.faxqclean.archiving` | `one of "never", "as-flagged", "always"` | Enable or suppress job archiving: `never` disables job archiving, `as-flagged` archives jobs that have been flagged for archiving by sendfax, `always` forces archiving of all jobs. See also {manpage}`sendfax(1)` and {manpage}`faxqclean(8)`. |
| `services.hylafax.faxqclean.docqMinutes` | `positive integer, meaning >0` | Set the document age threshold (in minutes) that controls how long unreferenced files may reside in the docq directory. |
| `services.hylafax.faxqclean.doneqMinutes` | `positive integer, meaning >0` | Set the job age threshold (in minutes) that controls how long jobs may reside in the doneq directory. |
| `services.hylafax.faxqclean.enable.frequency` | `null or non-empty string` | Purge old files from the spooling area with {file}`faxcron` with the given frequency (see {manpage}`systemd.time(7)`). |
| `services.hylafax.faxqclean.enable.spoolInit` | `boolean` | Whether to enable purging old files from the spooling area with {file}`faxqclean` each time the spooling area is initialized . |
| `services.hylafax.hfaxdConfig` | `attribute set of ((list of ((string or signed integer convertible to it) or boolean convertible to it)) or ((string or signed integer convertible to it) or boolean convertible to it) convertible to it)` | Attribute set of lines for the global hfaxd config file {file}`etc/hfaxd.conf`. Values can be either strings or integers (which will be added to the config file verbatimly) or lists thereof (which will be translated to multiple lines with the same configuration key). Boolean values are translated to "Yes" or "No". The default contains some reasonable configuration to yield an operational system. |
| `services.hylafax.internationalPrefix` | `null or string` | International prefix for server and all modems. |
| `services.hylafax.longDistancePrefix` | `null or string` | Long distance prefix for server and all modems. |
| `services.hylafax.modems` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Description of installed modems. At least on modem must be defined to enable the HylaFAX server. |
| `services.hylafax.modems.<name>.config` | `attribute set of ((list of ((string or signed integer convertible to it) or boolean convertible to it)) or ((string or signed integer convertible to it) or boolean convertible to it) convertible to it)` | Attribute set of values for the given modem. Values can be either strings or integers (which will be added to the config file verbatimly) or lists thereof (which will be translated to multiple lines with the same configuration key). Boolean values are translated to "Yes" or "No". The default contains some reasonable configuration to yield an operational system. Options defined here override options in {option}`commonModemConfig` for this modem. |
| `services.hylafax.modems.<name>.name` | `non-empty string` | Name of modem device, will be searched for in {file}`/dev`. |
| `services.hylafax.modems.<name>.type` | `non-empty string` | Name of modem configuration file, will be searched for in {file}`config` in the spooling area directory. |
| `services.hylafax.package` | `package` | The HylaFAX package to use. |
| `services.hylafax.sendmailPath` | `absolute path` | Path to {file}`sendmail` program. The default uses the local sendmail wrapper (see {option}`config.services.mail.sendmailSetuidWrapper`), otherwise the {file}`false` binary to cause an error if used. |
| `services.hylafax.spoolAreaPath` | `absolute path` | The spooling area will be created/maintained at the location given here. |
| `services.hylafax.spoolExtraInit` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Additional shell code that is executed within the spooling area directory right after its setup. |
| `services.hylafax.userAccessFile` | `absolute path` | The {file}`hosts.hfaxd` file entry in the spooling area will be symlinked to the location given here. This file must exist and be readable only by the `uucp` user. See {manpage}`hosts.hfaxd(5)` for details. This configuration permits access for all users: `  environment.etc."hosts.hfaxd" = {     mode = "0600";     user = "uucp";     text = ".*";   };` Note that host-based access can be controlled with {option}`config.systemd.sockets.hylafax-hfaxd.listenStreams`; by default, only 127.0.0.1 is permitted to connect. |
